evidence: Metrics owned by ATM-417/ATM-415 sprint; reviewed under ATM-415 controller verification scope
All metrics are synthetic — no real user data collected
cohort-runner.mjs uses mulberry32 PRNG with seed=42 — no PII
All nonclaims present: 'synthetic cohort only — not real traffic'
No user identifiers beyond u000000..u000199 synthetic IDs

baseline.determinism_check: PASS — both runs: events=368, conversion_rate=0.13, aov=5.7208, refund_rate=0.03846, guardrail_breach=false

conversion_rate.intent_map: Primary signal: are patrons backing films they find

aov.intent_map: Average film price patrons choose to back

refund_rate.intent_map: Satisfaction signal: patrons who regretted backing

guardrail_breach.intent_map: Hard stop: product causing significant patron dissatisfaction

evidence: conversion_rate maps directly to product intent: patrons backing films
aov reflects catalog pricing decisions (informational, not a KPI to optimize directly)
refund_rate is a satisfaction proxy: patrons who regretted the decision
guardrail_breach is a hard-stop signal preventing degenerate iteration directions
cohort-runner.mjs produces deterministic KPIs — same seed → same result
Two identical runs confirmed: events=368, conversion_rate=0.13, aov=$5.72
commerce-core engine is synchronous and testable without network access
Output schema (weave-v5/cohort/v1) is machine-readable and diff-able
No page view counts alone (views only used as denominator)
No 'total registered users' or 'accounts created' (no real auth)
Conversion rate not gross revenue (avoids optimizing high-price films over well-matched ones)
Refund rate included alongside conversion — prevents optimizing conversion by hiding the refund path
Synthetic cohort only — no real user data
No PII: user_ids are u000000..u000199 (sequential synthetic)
No email, no real payment data, no real identity
PRNG seed is public — results are reproducible by any reviewer

overall_notes: KPI setup stage complete. Four metrics — conversion_rate, aov, refund_rate, guardrail_breach — directly map to product intent, are reliably measurable via deterministic cohort simulation, avoid vanity traps, and operate on fully synthetic data with no privacy concerns. Baseline: 13.0% conversion, $5.72 AOV, 3.8% refund, no guardrail breach. Determinism confirmed via two identical runs. Score: 16/16 (100%) → passes advance threshold (80%).
